# Research — 2026-06-02

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents, permissions)

> Continuation of 2026-06-01. The 06-01 pass enumerated five gaps: extending
> per-dir AGENTS.md system-reminders to write/edit/multiedit/grep,
> `ALEXI_SESSION_ID`/`ALEXICODE` env injection for stdio MCP, `workdir` field
> on `SessionMetadata` + filter, plugin "rule contributions" surface, and a
> system-`rg` fast-path for `grep`. **All five are now in `src/`.** Verified:
> `instructionsForPath` is wired into `read.ts`, `write.ts`, `edit.ts`,
> `multiedit.ts`, and `grep.ts` with matching tests under
> `src/tool/tools/__tests__/*.agentsmd.test.ts`; `McpConnectOptions.sessionId`
> is plumbed and `ALEXI_SESSION_ID`/`ALEXICODE=1` are injected at
> `src/mcp/client.ts:175-178`; `SessionMetadata.workdir` exists at
> `src/core/sessionManager.ts:46` with the filter at `:212-238` and the
> `--here`/`--workdir`/`--all` flags shipped; `PluginRuleSchema` +
> `ResolvedPluginRule` + `runRuleCommand` are live in `src/plugin/index.ts`
> and `src/plugin/ruleCommandRunner.ts`; `src/tool/tools/grep.ts` has the
> `rg` fast-path with `ALEXI_DISABLE_RG=1` override. This pass integrates
> claude-code **v2.1.160** (Jun 2 — large), cline **v3.0.16-rc** (#11084,
> #11149, #11192, #11193, #11196 — May 31 → Jun 2), kilocode **v7.3.20-21**
> (May 30 → Jun 2 — most are TUI-only or Kilo-product specific), aider (no
> in-window functional commits), and continuedev (no in-window commits).

## Executive Summary

- **All 5 carry-overs from 2026-06-01 are closed.** Per-dir AGENTS.md
  system-reminders are now emitted by `read`/`write`/`edit`/`multiedit`/`grep`
  with isolated tests; `ALEXI_SESSION_ID`+`ALEXICODE=1` are injected into
  stdio MCP env at `src/mcp/client.ts:175-178`; `SessionMetadata.workdir`
  + `listSessions({ workdir })` filter shipped in `src/core/sessionManager.ts`;
  plugin rule contributions (`inline`/`file`/`command` sources, sandbox-bounded
  spawn helper, per-session caching) live in `src/plugin/`; `grep` and
  `codesearch` use a system-`rg` fast-path with `ALEXI_DISABLE_RG=1` opt-out.
- **claude-code v2.1.160 — write-prompt-on-shell-startup-files (large)**: the
  most consequential upstream change in the window. Writes to `~/.zshenv`,
  `~/.zlogin`, `~/.bash_login`, and `~/.config/git/` now prompt even in
  modes that would otherwise auto-allow, because *next-shell* execution makes
  them indistinguishable from arbitrary code execution. `acceptEdits`-style
  modes additionally prompt before writing build-tool config that grants
  install-time/CI-time execution: `.npmrc`, `.yarnrc*`, `bunfig.toml`,
  `.bazelrc`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.devcontainer/`, etc. We have
  `ConfigProtection` (`src/permission/config-paths.ts:8-28`) that protects
  `.kilo/`, `.alexi/`, `kilo.json`, `AGENTS.md` etc., but **none of the
  shell-startup or build-tool-config locations are listed there**. This is
  a real footgun: an agent in any auto-allow mode can write a `cat-token-to-curl`
  payload into `~/.zshenv` and the user's next shell session executes it
  silently. Item #1 below.
- **claude-code v2.1.160 — single-file `grep` satisfies read-before-edit**:
  upstream relaxed the read-before-edit constraint when the agent only
  needed to look at *one* file and used `grep`/`egrep`/`fgrep` to do it.
  We do not implement read-before-edit at all (`grep -rn` for that pattern
  in `src/` returns nothing), so this is informational — but worth noting
  if we ever add it.
- **claude-code v2.1.160 — file:/// hyperlink rewriting on Windows**: ours
  is hyperlink-emit (OSC-8) only, not hyperlink-consumption. Not applicable.
- **cline #11084 — `CLINE_PLUGIN_IMPORT_TIMEOUT_MS` env override**: cline
  exposed an env var to override the 4000ms plugin-sandbox import timeout
  because Windows cold starts blow through it. Our equivalent surface is
  `runRuleCommand`'s 5s `timeoutMs` (`src/plugin/index.ts:324` declares
  the per-rule override; `src/plugin/ruleCommandRunner.ts:215` enforces).
  We do not have a *global* env-var fallback when manifests don't declare
  one. Same Windows-cold-start problem applies. Item #2.
- **cline #11149 — typed `TimeoutError` for shell timeouts**: cline introduced
  a typed `TimeoutError` so timeout kills can be distinguished from generic
  command failures without changing the schema, and emitted SDK telemetry
  for `run_commands` timeouts. Our `bash.ts` already returns
  `BashResult.timedOut: boolean` (`src/tool/tools/bash.ts:106,193`) — the
  signal is *there*, but it's a flag on the data, not a typed error, and
  `webfetch.ts:138` uses string `'Request timed out or was aborted'` which
  collapses two different conditions. Cline's pattern (typed `TimeoutError`)
  is worth adopting in a small focused refactor: a single `TimeoutError`
  class in `src/utils/`, used by `bash`, `webfetch`, `browser`, `shell`, and
  `diagnostics`. Item #3.
- **cline #11192 — `env-blocker` example plugin via `beforeTool` hook**:
  cline shipped a *plugin example* that registers a `beforeTool` runtime
  hook returning `{ skip: true }` for any tool call that touches `.env*`
  (excluding `.env.example`/`.env.sample`/`.env.template`), covering both
  direct file tools and shell reads (`cat .env`, `source .env.production`).
  We already have a `deny-secrets` *permission rule*
  (`src/permission/index.ts:602-603`: `paths: ['**/.env', '**/.env.*',
   '**/secrets.*', '**/*credential*', '**/*secret*']`), but it's a *rule*,
  not a *plugin example*. The cline pattern matters: it shows users how
  to compose runtime safety policies as plugins, which is exactly the
  surface we just shipped via plugin rule contributions. Adding a parallel
  `examples/plugins/env-blocker/` (using a `PreToolUse` hook + `decision:
  'block'`) would (a) give plugin authors a concrete reference and (b)
  exercise our hook + plugin path end-to-end. Item #4.
- **cline #11196 — show MCP OAuth errors in TUI**: when an MCP server has
  `oauth.lastError`, cline's MCP list now shows the row as errored with
  the error detail, instead of looking like an ordinary enabled server. Our
  MCP client (`src/mcp/client.ts`) tracks `connection.status` and `error`
  (`:36-37`) but does not surface OAuth-specific failures distinctly, and
  no `oauth.lastError` field exists. Today our MCP UI is `/mcp`-style listing
  via `alexi` commands. Worth recording the OAuth error path as a follow-up
  when we add OAuth-MCP support, but **we don't currently support OAuth MCP
  servers at all** (only stdio + sse with manual headers). Defer.
- **cline #11193 — `/plugins` slash command**: opens settings on the
  Plugins tab. We don't have a Settings dialog the way cline does — our
  plugin surface is `alexi plugin <subcommand>` (`src/cli/commands/plugin.ts`)
  — but we *do* have an interactive REPL (`src/cli/interactive.ts:305`
  `handleCommand`) with slash commands (`/help`, `/model`, `/session`,
  `/agent`, `/status`). Adding `/plugins` (and `/skills`) as REPL commands
  that print the same info as `alexi plugin list` / `alexi skill list`,
  with a one-key toggle, would mirror the discoverability win. Item #5.
- **kilocode #10310 — subagent running spinner**: TUI-only fix for an
  `Ink`/`solid-js` style component. We don't have a subagent footer, but
  we do have a `Spinner` component (`src/cli/tui/components/Spinner.tsx`)
  with `agent.color` already wired. **Our `task` tool (subagents) has no
  visible activity indicator in the TUI when a subagent is running** —
  the parent session sits silent. Worth confirming via TUI smoke whether
  the parent spinner stays animated during subagent calls. Tracker.
- **kilocode #10796 — opt-in background-process port scans**
  (`KILO_BACKGROUND_PROCESS_PORTS=true`): our `background-process.ts`
  exists; check whether it polls listening ports continuously. If so, gate
  behind an env var the same way. Tracker until verified.
- **kilocode #10793 — clear agent variant overrides** (nullable schema
  patch): VS-Code-extension–specific. N/A.
- **kilocode #10298 (`kilo profile`)**, **#10191 (`\n` literal in
  DialogAlert)**, **#10306 (mouse-click checkbox toggle)**, **#9499
  (submodule worktree-family fix)**: TUI-product-specific or
  Kilo-Gateway-API-specific. N/A.
- **claude-code security-guidance plugin (May 26, just outside the
  7-day window but cross-cycle relevant)**: hooks-driven plugin that does
  on-edit pattern-based warnings (eval, pickle.loads, hardcoded creds,
  dangerouslySetInnerHTML, ~25 patterns), an end-of-turn LLM diff review
  for harder issues (auth bypass, IDOR, SSRF, weak crypto, unsafe deser,
  path traversal), and a deeper agentic review on `git commit`. Reads
  `CLAUDE-SECURITY-GUIDANCE.md` for org-specific rules. We just shipped
  plugin rule contributions and have `Stop`/`PreToolUse`/`PostToolUse`
  hooks — every primitive is in place to ship an analogous Alexi plugin
  example. Worth scoping as a separate work item rather than the research
  cycle's deliverable. Tracker.

## Detailed Findings

### What was closed since 2026-06-01 (verified in code)
- **Per-dir AGENTS.md system-reminders for write/edit/multiedit/grep** —
  tests in `src/tool/tools/__tests__/{write,edit,multiedit,grep}.agentsmd.test.ts`
  (each ~100 lines: emit-on-touch, no-emit-when-no-AGENTS.md, no-re-emit
  via shared `agentsMdSeen`, silent-when-context-omitted). For `grep`, the
  test asserts one reminder per *unique* AGENTS.md whose subtree contains
  matches (multiple matches under one subtree → one reminder).
- **`ALEXI_SESSION_ID` + `ALEXICODE=1` in stdio MCP env** —
  `src/mcp/client.ts:45-48` (`McpConnectOptions.sessionId`),
  `:175-178` injects all three vars *before* user `env` so user config can
  override (last-write semantics retained, comment at `:172-174`).
- **`workdir` on `SessionMetadata` + filter** —
  `src/core/sessionManager.ts:14-46` (normalize helper + optional field),
  `:92` (set on creation), `:212-238` (filter, with explicit handling for
  legacy `workdir === undefined` sessions: excluded from `--here`, kept
  in `--all`).
- **Plugin rule contributions** —
  `src/plugin/index.ts:301-340` (`PluginRuleSchema` zod definition: name,
  scope `'session'|'always'`, source `'inline'|'file'|'command'`),
  `:343-410` (`ResolvedPluginRule`), `:425-560` (`resolvePluginRule` for
  `inline`/`file` plus the `command` descriptor pass-through),
  `:565-620` (`runPluginRuleCommand` orchestrator with caching),
  `src/plugin/ruleCommandRunner.ts` (215-line spawn helper with bounded
  timeout, 32KB stdout cap, env scrubbing per `:72,284`),
  `:1225-1271` (`getEnabledRules`), `:1710-1728`
  (`resolvePluginRulesForPrompt` — parallel resolve at prompt-assembly time).
- **System-`rg` fast-path** — `src/tool/tools/grep.ts:4,57,64-66` (env
  override `ALEXI_DISABLE_RG=1`), `:572` (fast-path branch),
  `:526` (help text), `__tests__/grep.agentsmd.test.ts:10-23` (test pattern
  exercises the env opt-out for deterministic JS-walker behaviour).

### Still open from 2026-06-01
- None.

### New gaps (sorted by leverage)

#### 1. Add shell-startup-file + build-tool-config protection to `ConfigProtection` (Category: permission — small/medium, ~6h)
- **What**: Extend `src/permission/config-paths.ts` (or add a sibling
  module) so writes/edits to the following paths *always prompt* and
  cannot be allow-listed by `acceptEdits`-style auto-modes:
  - **Shell startup**: `~/.zshenv`, `~/.zlogin`, `~/.bashrc`,
    `~/.bash_login`, `~/.bash_profile`, `~/.profile`, `~/.cshrc`,
    `~/.kshrc`, `~/.tcshrc`, `~/.config/fish/config.fish`,
    `$ENV` (`ksh`), `~/.zshrc` (already implicitly executed every shell).
  - **Git config**: `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.config/git/config`,
    `~/.config/git/attributes`, `~/.config/git/ignore`. (Git config can
    install hooks via `core.hooksPath`, exec aliases via `[alias] x = !sh`.)
  - **Build-tool config that runs code on install/CI**: `.npmrc`
    (`scripts-prepend-node-path`, `script-shell`),
    `.yarnrc`/`.yarnrc.yml` (yarn `plugins`), `.pnpmfile.cjs`,
    `bunfig.toml`, `.bazelrc`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`,
    `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`, `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`,
    `.husky/`, `Makefile` *root only* (debatable — leave out).
  - **CI / hook directories** (write-only protection, reads fine):
    `.git/hooks/`, `.github/workflows/` (large blast radius for the next
    `gh actions` run).
- **Why**: claude-code v2.1.160 calls this out specifically as "could
  otherwise lead to unintended command execution". The mechanism is
  identical for us: an agent operating under any future "auto-edit-everything"
  permission mode can drop a payload into `~/.zshenv`, and the user's
  *next interactive shell* exfiltrates secrets to a webhook with zero
  agent involvement at execution time. Today our `ConfigProtection`
  protects only Alexi-flavoured config (`.kilo/`, `.alexi/`, `kilo.json`,
  `AGENTS.md`) and the project root's `kilo.json`-family files.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.160 changelog — "Added a prompt
  before writing to shell startup files (`.zshenv`, `.zlogin`,
  `.bash_login`) and `~/.config/git/`" + "`acceptEdits` mode now prompts
  before writing build-tool config files that grant code execution
  (`.npmrc`, `.yarnrc*`, `bunfig.toml`, `.bazelrc`,
  `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.devcontainer/`, etc.)".
- **Size**: small/medium (~6h). The existing module is the right
  insertion point and already has the `isAbsolute(absolutePath, projectRoot)`
  helper; we just need a second classifier `isShellOrBuildExecPath(p)`
  that consults a hardcoded path list (with `~` expansion via
  `os.homedir()` and Windows equivalents `%USERPROFILE%`,
  `%APPDATA%\\npm\\.npmrc`, etc.).
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - Add `SHELL_STARTUP_FILES` (set of basenames + home-relative paths)
    and `BUILD_TOOL_EXEC_FILES` (per-project basenames) constants in
    `config-paths.ts`.
  - Add `isShellStartup(absolutePath)` and `isBuildToolExec(relativeOrAbsolute)`
    that check (a) home-relative match for shell startup, (b) basename
    match anywhere in the workspace for build-tool exec files (npmrc /
    yarnrc / bunfig appear at any depth in monorepos).
  - Extend `ConfigProtection.isRequest()` to return `true` for these as
    well. Add a new metadata field `metadata.execRisk: 'shell' |
    'build-tool' | 'ci'` so the permission UI can render a stronger
    warning ("This file is executed at next shell startup").
  - In a future PR (out of scope here): when we add an `acceptEdits`-style
    mode, gate strictly through this classifier — never allow
    auto-approval for `execRisk` paths.
  - Tests: tmp `HOME`, write `~/.zshenv`, assert `isShellStartup` and
    `isRequest` both fire; same for `.npmrc` at project root and at
    `apps/web/.npmrc`; negative case for `.zshrc.example` and
    `.npmrc.template`.

#### 2. Global env-var override for `runRuleCommand` timeout (Category: plugin — small, < 2h)
- **What**: Today every plugin-rule command times out at the per-rule
  `timeoutMs` (default 5s, settable per rule via `command.timeoutMs` —
  see `src/plugin/index.ts:324`). Add an env-var override
  `ALEXI_PLUGIN_RULE_TIMEOUT_MS` that applies when the rule does not set
  its own. Precedence: `rule.command.timeoutMs > env > 5000`.
- **Why**: cline #11084 (Jun 2) shipped exactly this for their plugin
  sandbox import phase because Windows cold starts exceeded the
  hardcoded 4000ms — and the failure mode was *every plugin silently
  failing to register*. Our parallel: a slow-starting rule command
  (e.g. one that fetches a piece of context from a remote service) will
  silently produce empty content for the system prompt, with the failure
  showing up only in `runPluginRuleCommand`'s warning log. The env-var
  is the smallest possible blast-radius fix and matches the cline
  precedent semantically (no default change, no new CLI surface).
- **Source**: cline/cline#11084 — "expose CLINE_PLUGIN_IMPORT_TIMEOUT_MS
  env override".
- **Size**: small (< 2h). One env-read at module load + a one-line
  precedence change in `runRuleCommand`.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  ```ts
  // src/plugin/ruleCommandRunner.ts
  const ENV_TIMEOUT = (() => {
    const raw = process.env.ALEXI_PLUGIN_RULE_TIMEOUT_MS;
    const n = raw ? parseInt(raw, 10) : NaN;
    return Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0 ? n : undefined;
  })();
  // ...inside runRuleCommand:
  const timeoutMs = opts.timeoutMs ?? ENV_TIMEOUT ?? 5000;
  ```
  - Test: set `ALEXI_PLUGIN_RULE_TIMEOUT_MS=150`, run a rule command
    that sleeps 1s, assert the timeout fires at ~150ms not 5000ms.

#### 3. Typed `TimeoutError` for shell/network timeouts (Category: tool — small, ~3h)
- **What**: Add `class TimeoutError extends Error` in `src/utils/errors.ts`
  (or extend an existing errors module) with a `kind: 'TimeoutError'`
  brand. Refactor `bash.ts`, `shell.ts`, `webfetch.ts`, `diagnostics.ts`,
  `browser.ts` to emit/recognise this typed error. `BashResult.timedOut:
  boolean` stays for back-compat; downstream consumers gain the option
  to receive a typed error instead.
- **Why**: cline #11149 (Jun 1) documented why this matters: timeout
  *kills* are operationally different from a process exiting non-zero.
  Today our hooks/permission flow can't distinguish the two — `bash.ts`
  returns `success: false, error: 'Command exited with code -1'` for
  *both* a SIGTERM-from-timeout and a process that crashed with
  `process.exit(-1)`. A typed error lets the agent retry-with-longer-timeout
  for one and not the other. It also gives us a clean place to emit the
  one telemetry signal cline added (`tool_timeout` events with
  `effective_timeout_ms`, `timeout_source`, `command_count`,
  `duration_ms`) when/if we wire OTEL.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11149 — "Add SDK telemetry for run_commands
  timeouts" (introduces typed `TimeoutError`).
- **Size**: small (~3h). The plumbing is small; the test surface is the
  larger half (one test per tool that already has a `timedOut`/timeout
  path).
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - Add `src/utils/errors.ts` with `class TimeoutError extends Error
    { readonly kind = 'TimeoutError' as const; constructor(public
    readonly effectiveTimeoutMs: number, public readonly tool: string,
    message?: string) { super(message ?? \`${tool} timed out after
    ${effectiveTimeoutMs}ms\`); this.name = 'TimeoutError'; } }`.
  - In `bash.ts:138-146` (the `setTimeout` callback that triggers
    `timedOut = true`), keep `timedOut` on the result *and* attach
    `result.timeoutError = new TimeoutError(timeout, 'bash')`.
  - Same in `webfetch.ts:138` (replace string error), `shell.ts`,
    `diagnostics.ts`, `browser.ts:232,276` (already has `err.name ===
    'TimeoutError'` checks; convert to `instanceof TimeoutError`).
  - `agenticChat.ts` (or wherever tool-error → user-message conversion
    lives) gets a single branch that recognises the typed error and
    emits a clearer model-facing message ("The command timed out after
    Xs. Consider passing a longer `timeout` argument.").

#### 4. Ship an `examples/plugins/env-blocker/` reference plugin (Category: plugin — small, ~4h)
- **What**: Add a working `examples/plugins/env-blocker/` that
  demonstrates a `PreToolUse` hook (or skill plugin contribution)
  returning `decision: 'block'` for any tool call that touches
  secret-bearing `.env*` files (excluding `.env.example`,
  `.env.sample`, `.env.template`). Cover:
  - File tools (`read`, `edit`, `multiedit`, `write`, `delete`,
    `apply_patch`) by inspecting the `filepath` parameter.
  - Shell tools (`bash`, `shell`, `background-process`) by parsing
    the `command` for `cat .env`, `source .env.production`,
    `head .env.local`, etc. (Use `src/permission/shell-parser.ts`
    which is already plumbed for this kind of analysis.)
- **Why**: cline #11192 shipped exactly this plugin example and the
  blog-post-grade rationale ("a rule in AGENTS.md is a suggestion the
  model is free to ignore; this is execution-path enforcement") is
  literally the case for our plugin rule contributions surface that
  shipped this cycle. The implementation already has analogues:
  `src/skill/customize-alexi.ts:67` declares `paths: ['**/.env',
  '**/secrets.*']` for the customize-alexi skill, and
  `src/permission/index.ts:602-603` declares the same as a rule. What
  we're missing is the *plugin author–facing demonstration* that wires
  these primitives into a runnable `plugin.json` — i.e. a worked
  example. The plugin scaffolder shipped in the prior cycle (`alexi
  plugin init`) is the obvious entry point: `alexi plugin init
  env-blocker --template=env-blocker` could even pull from a built-in
  template directory.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11192 — "docs(sdk): add env-blocker plugin
  example". Companion: claude-code's broader pattern of shipping
  hook-based execution-path policies (security-guidance plugin).
- **Size**: small (~4h). Most of the work is the example + README;
  the runtime hook surface already exists.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - Create `examples/plugins/env-blocker/plugin.json` declaring a
    `PreToolUse` hook entry (script path + matcher).
  - Create `examples/plugins/env-blocker/hooks/block-env.ts` that
    parses tool input shapes (single string, array of strings,
    `{ command }`, etc.) and returns `{ decision: 'block', reason:
    'env-blocker: ${path} is a secret file; allow only template/sample
    variants' }` for any path that matches the deny list.
  - Cover both file-tool and shell paths. Use
    `src/permission/shell-parser.ts:parseCommand` for the shell side.
  - Add a README with the same talking points cline shipped (rule vs.
    enforcement, blast-radius story).
  - Wire `alexi plugin init env-blocker --template=env-blocker` if
    cheap; otherwise just document the manual install path.
  - Tests: `tests/plugin/env-blocker.example.test.ts` exercising both
    a `read` of `.env` and a `bash cat .env` against the example
    plugin loaded into a temp `~/.alexi/skills/`.

#### 5. `/plugins` and `/skills` REPL slash commands (Category: command — small, ~3h)
- **What**: Add `/plugins` and `/skills` to the interactive REPL
  (`src/cli/interactive.ts:305-368`). Each prints a compact list
  identical to `alexi plugin list` / `alexi skill list` (name, version,
  enabled state) plus a per-row footer like *"toggle: /plugins toggle
  <name>"*. `/plugins toggle <name>` flips `pluginState.enabled` and
  fires the existing `reloadSkills` plumbing
  (`src/hooks/index.ts:555-562`).
- **Why**: cline #11193 (Jun 1) shipped `/plugins` *because* the
  configuration was reachable only via the CLI subcommand — discovery
  was poor. Our state today is the same: a user inside the interactive
  REPL has to `Ctrl-Z`, run `alexi plugin list`, and `fg` to see what's
  loaded. We just shipped plugin auto-load and rule contributions; this
  cycle's small-win is making them discoverable from where users
  actually live (the REPL).
- **Source**: cline/cline#11193 — "feat(cli): add plugins slash
  command" (opens settings on Plugins tab; equivalent UX).
- **Size**: small (~3h). The data already exists — `getEnabledRules()`
  on the plugin manager, the CLI subcommand prints similar output.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - Add cases to `handleCommand` in `src/cli/interactive.ts:305`:
    ```ts
    case 'plugins': {
      const { listPlugins, togglePlugin } = await import('./commands/plugin.js');
      if (args[0] === 'toggle' && args[1]) {
        const next = await togglePlugin(args[1]);
        console.log(c('green', `\n  ${args[1]} → ${next ? 'enabled' : 'disabled'}\n`));
      } else {
        await listPlugins({ format: 'table' });
      }
      return true;
    }
    case 'skills': { /* analogous via skill manager */ }
    ```
  - Update `printHelp()` (`src/cli/interactive.ts:136`-area) to add the
    two new commands.
  - Tests: REPL command-dispatch unit tests under
    `src/cli/__tests__/interactive.commands.test.ts`.

### Notable upstream items deliberately deferred
- **claude-code v2.1.160 read-before-edit relaxation** — we never
  required read-before-edit. No action.
- **claude-code v2.1.160 background-session lifecycle fixes (`claude
  rm`, idle reap, sigterm-then-sigkill ordering, etc.)** — depends on
  a background-session daemon we don't have.
- **claude-code v2.1.160 vim-mode `p`/`v$` register fix, brief-mode
  scrollback fix, voice-mode non-ASCII project paths** — features we
  don't ship.
- **claude-code v2.1.160 Windows file:/// hyperlink rewriting fix** —
  we emit hyperlinks via OSC-8 (which doesn't have this bug); we don't
  consume.
- **claude-code v2.1.160 dynamic-workflows trigger keyword
  `workflow → ultracode`** — orchestration product feature, no parity.
- **cline #11196 MCP OAuth `lastError` in TUI** — we don't ship OAuth
  MCP support yet.
- **cline #11195 bundle Cline Hub dashboard** — out of envelope (web).
- **cline #11149 SDK telemetry for `run_commands`** — capture *the
  typed error* (Item #3) but defer the OTEL emission until we have a
  consumer. Today no `src/` module imports OpenTelemetry.
- **kilocode #10310 subagent-running spinner** — confirm during a TUI
  smoke that our parent-session spinner stays animated through `task`
  invocations. If not, port the pattern. Tracker only — file a small
  issue rather than research action.
- **kilocode #10796 background-process port-scan opt-in** — verify
  whether `src/tool/tools/background-process.ts` does periodic port
  scans; if so, gate behind `ALEXI_BACKGROUND_PROCESS_PORTS=true`. If
  it doesn't, no action. Tracker.
- **kilocode #10793 nullable agent-variant schema** — VS-Code-extension
  config-schema fix, not portable.
- **kilocode #10298 `kilo profile`**, **#10191 DialogAlert `\n`**,
  **#10306 mouse-click checkbox**, **#9499 submodule worktree-family
  `git worktree list` parsing** — TUI/Kilo-Gateway-product-specific. N/A.
- **POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell permission audit (deferred from
  2026-05-29 → 2026-06-01)** — still not on a tracked ticket. Carry
  forward.
- **claude-code security-guidance plugin port to Alexi** — every
  primitive (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, plugin rule contributions)
  is now in `src/`. This is a separate body of work, not a research
  finding. Track as a multi-PR initiative, not a checklist item.
- **continuedev (no in-window functional commits — last functional
  commit Apr 17 in `add-mercury-2`)** — nothing to act on this cycle.
- **aider (no in-window functional commits — model-list expansion only,
  May 22)** — nothing to act on this cycle.

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Land the small-wins cycle (~1 day total)**:
   - **Item #2** — `ALEXI_PLUGIN_RULE_TIMEOUT_MS` env override. Tiny
     change, real Windows correctness benefit, follows the cline
     precedent exactly.
   - **Item #3** — Typed `TimeoutError`. Self-contained: one new errors
     module + 5 call-site refactors + one test per call site.
   - **Item #5** — `/plugins` and `/skills` REPL commands. Self-contained,
     reuses existing CLI command code.
   These three land as separate PRs (different directories, no
   coupling). Total effort ~1 day with tests.

2. **Item #1 (shell-startup + build-tool config protection) is the
   marquee work for this cycle**. It is the only finding that closes
   a real security gap rather than a quality-of-life one. Plan for
   ~6h: classifier module, integration into `ConfigProtection`,
   metadata field for the permission UI to render a stronger warning,
   and a thorough test matrix (each protected basename × at-root vs.
   monorepo-nested vs. home-relative). This *must* land before any
   future `acceptEdits`-style auto-mode work.

3. **Item #4 (`env-blocker` reference plugin)** is the cycle's
   medium-effort write-up-friendly deliverable. Pair it with a short
   README that doubles as the "how to write an Alexi plugin" worked
   example for outside contributors. The plugin scaffolder
   (`alexi plugin init`) shipped in the prior cycle is the natural
   home for `--template=env-blocker`.

4. **Open a tracking issue for the security-guidance plugin port**.
   All the building blocks (PreToolUse for on-edit pattern checks,
   Stop for end-of-turn diff review, plugin rule contributions for
   org policy via `ALEXI-SECURITY-GUIDANCE.md`) are in `src/`. This
   is a 1-2 week initiative, not a research finding — but it deserves
   to be on the roadmap.

5. **File the deferred POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell permission audit
   issue** if it still hasn't been opened (carry-over from 2026-05-29
   → 2026-06-01 → now). It is not getting smaller and it's the last
   open structural item in the permission subsystem.

6. **Verify `background-process` port-scan behaviour** (kilocode
   #10796). If we periodically scan, gate behind
   `ALEXI_BACKGROUND_PROCESS_PORTS=true`. If we don't, close the
   tracker.

7. **TUI smoke-check the subagent activity indicator** (kilocode
   #10310). If the parent-session spinner stays animated through `task`
   tool invocations, no action. If not, emit a child spinner with
   `agent.color` from the active subagent.

8. **Tracker hygiene**: The 2026-06-01 cycle's five "new gaps" are now
   zero. Two consecutive cycles at zero carry-overs. The pattern is
   now repeatable enough to be worth surfacing in the next planning
   doc as a velocity signal.

9. **Re-evaluate next cycle**: SAP-AI-Core auto-mode classifier
   availability, Opus 4.8 SAP id, OTEL instrumentation demand (Item
   #3 will give us a clean emit point if/when), plugin marketplace
   timeline.
